name: Perplexity API
what_it_does: Search-augmented LLM API that grounds responses in real-time web search. Provides up-to-date information with citations, combining LLM reasoning with live web search for factual, current answers.
use_cases: Agent workflows requiring up-to-date information beyond LLM training cutoff
Research automation with cited sources for fact-checked content generation
Competitive intelligence and market research with real-time data
News monitoring and current event summarization for business agents
Answering questions about recent events, prices, or rapidly changing information
not_for: Creative writing or tasks where grounding in web search is unnecessary
Tasks requiring large context windows for document analysis
High-volume generation where web search adds cost and latency
best_when: An agent needs current, factual answers with citations where the LLM's training data may be stale — especially for research, news monitoring, or real-time business intelligence.
avoid_when: Your task doesn't require current information or citations, and you can use a cheaper LLM without web search.

auth.notes: Simple Bearer token authentication. OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint. Easy migration from OpenAI SDK by changing base URL and model name.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free API tier; Perplexity Pro subscription gives some API credits
pricing.paid_tiers: Sonar ~$1/1M input, $1/1M output + $5/1000 search queries; Sonar Pro $3/1M input + $5/1000 searches
pricing.notes: Search queries are charged separately from token generation. Costs can add up quickly if every response triggers a search. Pro subscription ($20/month) includes some API credits.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by plan; not publicly specified

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAI-compatible error format. Errors are standard but documentation on specific Perplexity error scenarios is limited. Rate limit documentation is sparse.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Stateless chat completions API. Retrying is safe — may return different results due to live search and non-deterministic generation.
known_agent_gotchas: Search query costs (per-query) can make high-volume usage expensive
Web search introduces latency — responses are slower than non-search LLMs
Citations are returned in response but format varies — agents must parse citations array
Search may be triggered even for queries that don't need current info — increases cost
Model availability changes — not all models support search, check model-specific docs
Rate limits not clearly documented — agents may hit limits without clear error guidance
